steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The stock increases by 1% of its value the previous year — this is exponential (compound) growth, not linear. The model is $V(t) = 240(1 + 0.01)^t = 240(1.01)^t$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$240(1.01t)$': linear in $t$ — multiplies by a fixed amount each year, not a fixed percent. ✗
  • Choice B — '$240(1.1t)$': also linear in $t$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$240(1.01)^t$': exponential with base $1.01$, representing 1% growth per year. ✓
  • Choice D — '$240(1.1)^t$': exponential, but base $1.1$ is 10% growth per year, not 1%. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
